title: celtis laevigata var. smallii
intro: Celtis laevigata var. smallii, also known as the Texas hackberry or small hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Celtis laevigata var. smallii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal preparations to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
